scores.label: Shower / wetroom
scores.note: Shower likely in annexe — confirm type

scores.label: Physical separation
scores.note: Attached annexe — smoke separation needs confirming

scores.label: Budget headroom
scores.note: £5,000 under budget — no room for works

questions: Does the annexe have its own completely separate external entrance?
Is the annexe shower a walk-in/level-access shower, or does it have a step or bath?
Is there full air/smoke separation between the annexe and main house so a smoker in the annexe cannot affect the main living areas?
What is the heating fuel — oil, gas, LPG, heat pump or electric? What are approximate annual costs?
Is the drainage mains or private (septic tank / sewage treatment plant)?
What broadband speeds are available — specifically upload as well as download? Is full fibre (FTTP) available?

scores.label: Shower / wetroom
scores.note: Shower likely in annexe — confirm type

scores.label: Planning / building control
scores.note: No BC certificate — indemnity policy offered

scores.label: Fuel type
scores.note: Oil CH + wood burners — ASHP conversion recommended

scores.label: Drainage type
scores.note: Private drainage — septic/STP; check EA compliance

scores.label: Road noise
scores.note: Property positioned directly on A303 — assess at viewing

questions: Does the annexe have its own completely separate external entrance?
Is the annexe shower a walk-in/level-access shower, or does it have a step or bath?
What are the approximate annual oil heating costs? When was the oil boiler last serviced?
Is the private drainage a traditional septic tank or a sewage treatment plant? When was it last emptied and has it been assessed as compliant with the 2020 EA General Binding Rules?
Is there an onward chain on the vendor's side? Are the vendors already under offer elsewhere?
How far is the property set back from the A303 carriageway? Is road noise significant inside the house or in the garden, particularly from HGVs?
red_flags: No building control completion certificate — check indemnity policy covers adequately
Private drainage — budget £200–£400 for condition survey; up to £20k if replacement needed

scores.label: Physical separation
scores.note: Attached annexe — smoke separation needs confirming

scores.label: Listed building
scores.note: Grade II listed — alterations need Listed Building Consent

questions: Does the annexe have its own completely separate external entrance?
Does the annexe have a walk-in shower or wet room? If not, is there space to add one?
Is there full air/smoke separation between the annexe and main house so a smoker in the annexe cannot affect the main living areas?
Is there a walk-in or level-access shower — no step — accessible from the ground floor?
What is the heating fuel — oil, gas, LPG, heat pump or electric? What are approximate annual costs?
Is the drainage mains or private (septic tank / sewage treatment plant)?
What broadband speeds are available — specifically upload as well as download? Is full fibre (FTTP) available?
Are there any outstanding listed building consent issues or works carried out without consent? Has an LBC application been made for any works?
Is there an onward chain on the vendor's side? Are the vendors already under offer elsewhere?
red_flags: Grade II listed — ASHP, solar panels and annexe works all need Listed Building Consent (LBC)

questions: Is the annexe shower a step-in bath/shower or is there space to replace it with a level-access walk-in shower?
Has the annexe been granted planning permission and building control approval? Does it have its own council tax band?
Is there full air/smoke separation between the annexe and main house — i.e. can a smoker in the annexe not affect main house air quality?
Are all the annexe rooms on a single level (ground floor) with no internal stairs?
Is there space in the annexe bathroom to replace the bath with a level-access wet room?
Is the annexe heated by the same gas central heating as the main house, or does it have separate heating?
What broadband speeds are available — is full fibre (FTTP) available? What are upload speeds?
Is there an onward chain on the vendor's side?

questions: Does the annexe have its own completely separate external entrance?
Does the annexe have a walk-in shower or wet room? If not, is there space to add one?
Is there full air/smoke separation between the annexe and main house so a smoker in the annexe cannot affect the main living areas?
Is there a walk-in or level-access shower — no step — accessible from the ground floor?
What is the heating fuel — oil, gas, LPG, heat pump or electric? What are approximate annual costs?
Is the drainage mains or private (septic tank / sewage treatment plant)?
What broadband speeds are available — specifically upload as well as download? Is full fibre (FTTP) available?
Are there any outstanding listed building consent issues or works carried out without consent? Has an LBC application been made for any works?
Is there an onward chain on the vendor's side? Are the vendors already under offer elsewhere?
red_flags: Grade II listed — ASHP, solar panels and annexe works all need Listed Building Consent (LBC)

scores.label: Physical separation
scores.note: No annexe yet — separation depends on what is created

scores.label: Budget headroom
scores.note: £105,000 under budget — good scope for works

scores.label: Ferry dependency
scores.note: Isle of Wight — ferry required for all mainland access

questions: What is the realistic scope for creating a self-contained annexe — outbuilding, garage conversion or extension?
Is there a ground floor bedroom accessible without stairs, for a person with limited mobility?
What is the heating fuel — oil, gas, LPG, heat pump or electric? What are approximate annual costs?
What broadband speeds are available — specifically upload as well as download? Is full fibre (FTTP) available?
Is there an onward chain on the vendor's side? Are the vendors already under offer elsewhere?
red_flags: FERRY DEPENDENCY — medical access, moving day and repeat visits all require ferry; significant ongoing cost and logistical complexity for elderly relative
